summary: open-agents-ai ships postinstall daemon performing system reconnaissance and HTTP exfiltration
details: package.json declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks that invoke curl and launch dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs. The daemon imports os, child_process, fs, and http, calls os.userInfo() and execSync() to gather host identity and run system commands (including ping for network reconnaissance), and issues outbound http.request GETs carrying host identifiers. This is the canonical install-time host-fingerprint-and-beacon pattern: lifecycle-triggered execution, no consent, system info collected via os.userInfo() and execSync, then transmitted over HTTP to an external endpoint. The combination of preinstall+postinstall curl invocations plus a sibling daemon that execs system commands and POSTs/GETs host identity to a remote host is not a legitimate install workflow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
